meta.note: Edit this file to update the live site. No HTML/CSS edits needed for content changes.

landing.tagline: Interested in complex problems, simple solutions, and the messy process in between.
landing.intro: I enjoy working on problems that don't come with clear answers. Whether it's understanding users, navigating technical constraints, or exploring business opportunities, I like breaking down complexity, finding patterns, and building practical solutions. My experiences across engineering, design, and product development have taught me to approach challenges from multiple perspectives and stay comfortable in ambiguity.
landing.current_role: UX Designer & Business Analyst, Atlas Copco GECIA

projects.intro: Five things I've spent real hours on — some shipped, some still half-finished in a notebook somewhere.

items.title: DiaBuddy
items.subtitle: A digital platform for diabetes patients
items.description: A self-care platform for diabetes patients, built on user personas, journey maps, and a lot of How-Might-We questions.

items.title: TireTracker
items.subtitle: Bicycle management for campuses
items.description: A feature for managing the chaos of campus cycle-sharing, born from research into why bikes pile up and quietly disappear.

items.title: MunshiG
items.subtitle: Conversational expense tracking
items.description: Keep your big and small expenses tracked, without hassle.

items.title: Pain Relief Massager
items.subtitle: Rethinking period pain relief devices
items.description: Rethinking and improving period pain relief devices.

items.title: Washing Device for Reusable Cloth Pads
items.subtitle: Why — and where — the need exists
items.description: Understanding why, and where, the need for it exists.

side_quests.intro: Filed under: crochet, ink, things made for no reason but joy.

items.title: Crochet & Stuff
items.description: Yarn, hooks, and the occasional gift someone didn't know they needed.

items.title: Illustrations
items.description: Photos, captions, and the stories behind them — on the way.
